understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete KA59 by solving each level: for every level, move/click to control selectable pieces and position them so that every goal tile marked '+' in the semantic grid is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, which triggers WIN and advances to the next level until the final win.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn you control one selected piece (shown as 'P' and identified by player.id/position). MOVE_* attempts to shift that selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction; movement can be prevented by walls ('#'), blocks ('B'), boundaries, or other level-specific collision rules, and the movement_blocked hints only indicate adjacent objects and are not guaranteed outcomes. Some objects are pushable blocks: if a block is directly adjacent in the movement direction, a push may occur with the piece and block moving together, but walls/boundaries can stop either. If there are multiple selectables, you switch control using CLICK with the exact pixel/grid position of the other selectable (as listed in objects/selectables); clicking an invalid/empty/non-selectable position has no effect. The step budget is finite per level, so inefficient moves or failed clicks can exhaust steps before reaching the required configuration.
